message:
feat: align SQL schema references across all crates, harden SQL injection surface, and fix edge-case panics

WHY — The codebase had accumulated schema drift: queries in vc_cli,
vc_mcp, vc_query, vc_web, and vc_collect referenced table/column names
that no longer matched the canonical schema (e.g. "alerts" vs
"alert_history", "collector_status" vs "collector_health",
"health_scores" vs "health_summary", "machine_registry" vs "machines",
"ntm_sessions" vs "agent_sessions", "caut_usage" vs
"account_usage_snapshots").  Several string-interpolated SQL queries
were also vulnerable to SQL injection via unsanitized user input.
Additionally, a handful of numeric edge cases could cause panics or
silent data suppression.

SCHEMA ALIGNMENT (across vc_cli, vc_mcp, vc_query, vc_web, vc_collect):

- machines table: renamed columns status -> enabled (bool),
  last_seen -> last_seen_at, health_score -> tags; updated every query
  and template that referenced the old names.
- alert_history: all references to the old "alerts" table now use
  "alert_history"; "acked_at" -> "acknowledged_at" /
  "resolved_at IS NULL" for open-alert filtering.
- collector_health: "collector_status" -> "collector_health",
  "collector_name" -> "collector", "checked_at" -> "collected_at",
  "status = 'healthy'" -> "success = true", added freshness_seconds
  logic for stale-collector detection.
- health_summary: "health_scores" -> "health_summary",
  "computed_at" -> "collected_at".
- sys_samples: "sysmoni_snapshots" -> "sys_samples",
  "system_metrics_snapshots" -> "sys_samples"; query templates updated
  to use the actual columns (cpu_total, load1/5/15, mem_used_bytes,
  mem_total_bytes, mem_available_bytes).
- collector table mappings in vc_collect::node: "ntm_sessions" ->
  "ntm_sessions_snapshot", "afsc_snapshots" -> "afsc_status_snapshot",
  "cloud_benchmark_results" -> "cloud_bench_raw"; tests updated.
- NL query generation (vc_query::nl): "ntm_sessions" ->
  "agent_sessions", "caut_usage" -> "account_usage_snapshots",
  "amount_usd" -> "cost_estimate"; collector status query now uses
  "collector", "success", "collected_at", "rows_inserted", "error_class".
- repo_status_snapshots (001_initial_schema.sql): refactored columns
  to repo_id FK, dirty, ahead, behind, modified_count, untracked_count;
  added new repos inventory table.
- sys_samples: added mem_available_bytes, swap_total_bytes, core_count
  columns.

SQL INJECTION HARDENING (vc_store, vc_mcp, vc_cli, vc_query, vc_web):

- Introduced escape_sql_identifier() for table/column names used inside
  double-quoted identifiers; marked #[must_use].
- Replaced raw string interpolation with escape_sql_literal() in:
  * vc_cli alert-watch timestamp query
  * vc_mcp machine filter, severity filter, session/incident filter
  * vc_query cost builder (was using manual replace('\'', "''"))
  * vc_store incident replay (machines, alerts, audit, collector
    queries around at_ts), poll_schedule_decisions, sys_profile_samples,
    digest_reports, redaction_events, node_ingest_log,
    alert_routing_events, table_row_count, generic table export,
    guess_timestamp_column (both variants)
  * vc_web machine_by_id_handler, machine_collectors_handler

- vc_query::lib health-score persistence rewritten from raw
  format!("INSERT OR REPLACE ...") with string-interpolated values to
  structured serde_json::json!() rows passed through upsert_json(),
  eliminating an entire class of injection vectors.

TRANSACTION SAFETY (vc_store):

- insert_json_batch: wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT with ROLLBACK on any row
  failure; previously each row was an independent insert_json() call so
  a mid-batch failure left partial data.
- upsert_json: same BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK treatment; also added an
  explicit error if a non-Object JSON value is encountered (previously
  silently skipped).

EDGE-CASE / PANIC FIXES:

- vc_cli::toon::pct(): NaN guard (returns 0) and clamp to prevent
  f64-to-u32 overflow on extreme scores.
- vc_cli::toon::abbreviate() and watch::WatchEvent display: replaced
  byte-offset slicing (&s[..n]) with .chars().take(n) to prevent panic
  on multi-byte UTF-8 strings.
- vc_oracle::dna anomaly detection: enforced minimum effective stddev
  (max of 5% of mean or 0.001) so that zero-variance histories no
  longer silently suppress anomalies.
- vc_oracle::evolution genome randomisation: guarded against min >= max
  ranges in Float/Int genes and empty options Vec in Choice genes to
  prevent rand panic on empty ranges.

SSH PORTABILITY (vc_collect::ssh):

- base64 decode command now tries `base64 -d`, `base64 --decode`, and
  `base64 -D` (macOS) in a brace-group fallback chain, so file uploads
  work on both GNU and BSD/macOS targets.

WEBSOCKET ROBUSTNESS (vc_web):

- ws_session loop rewritten with tokio::select! to simultaneously await
  the heartbeat interval tick and incoming client messages; previously
  a disconnected client would never be detected between ticks, leaking
  the task until the next send failed.

NEW MIGRATIONS:

- 025_collector_tables.sql: creates beads_triage_snapshots,
  beads_graph_metrics, pt_processes, pt_snapshots, and
  gh_repo_issue_pr_snapshot tables that were referenced in collector
  code but never defined in the schema.
- 026_machine_status.sql: adds a "status" TEXT column (online/offline/
  unknown) to the machines table, distinct from the enabled boolean.
- Both migrations registered in the MIGRATIONS array in migrations.rs.

SCHEMA REGISTRY (vc_store::schema):

- Added REPOS, BEADS_TRIAGE_SNAPSHOTS, BEADS_GRAPH_METRICS,
  PT_PROCESSES, PT_SNAPSHOTS, GH_REPO_ISSUE_PR_SNAPSHOT table name
  constants.

ISSUE TRACKER (.beads/issues.jsonl):

- Added new issue entries tracking the schema alignment and collector
  table work.

TEST UPDATES:

- vc_collect::node tests updated for new table name mappings.
- vc_web tests updated to use collector_health table with new column
  names (collector, collected_at, success).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -84,7 +84,7 @@ fn build_fleet_section(store: &VcStore, summary: &mut DigestSummary) -> DigestSe
 
     // Count machines
     let machines: usize = store
-        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT machine_id) FROM machine_registry")
+        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT machine_id) FROM machines")
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
@@ -93,19 +93,19 @@ fn build_fleet_section(store: &VcStore, summary: &mut DigestSummary) -> DigestSe
 
     // Health scores
     let healthy: usize = store
-        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_scores WHERE overall_score >= 80")
+        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_summary WHERE overall_score >= 80")
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
     let degraded: usize = store
         .query_scalar::<i64>(
-            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_scores WHERE overall_score < 80 AND overall_score >= 50",
+            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_summary WHERE overall_score < 80 AND overall_score >= 50",
         )
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
     let critical: usize = store
-        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_scores WHERE overall_score < 50")
+        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_summary WHERE overall_score < 50")
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
@@ -127,7 +127,7 @@ fn build_alert_section(store: &VcStore, summary: &mut DigestSummary) -> DigestSe
     let mut items = Vec::new();
 
     let open: usize = store
-        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM alert_history WHERE status = 'open'")
+        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM alert_history WHERE resolved_at IS NULL")
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
@@ -158,12 +158,12 @@ fn build_collector_section(store: &VcStore, summary: &mut DigestSummary) -> Dige
     let mut items = Vec::new();
 
     let healthy: usize = store
-        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collector_health WHERE status = 'healthy'")
+        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collector_health WHERE success = true AND (freshness_seconds IS NULL OR freshness_seconds <= 600)")
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
     let stale: usize = store
-        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collector_health WHERE status = 'stale'")
+        .query_scalar::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collector_health WHERE freshness_seconds > 600")
         .ok()
         .and_then(|value| usize::try_from(value).ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);

@@ -154,7 +154,7 @@ impl QueryValidator {
         self.register_template(QueryTemplate {
             name: "machine_status".to_string(),
             description: "Get status of all machines or a specific machine".to_string(),
-            sql: "SELECT machine_id, hostname, status, last_seen, health_score \
+            sql: "SELECT machine_id, hostname, enabled, last_seen_at, tags \
                   FROM machines \
                   WHERE ({machine_id} IS NULL OR machine_id = {machine_id}) \
                   ORDER BY hostname \
@@ -181,7 +181,7 @@ impl QueryValidator {
         self.register_template(QueryTemplate {

-            sql: "SELECT id, rule_id, fired_at, severity, title, message, acked_at \
+            sql: "SELECT id, rule_id, fired_at, severity, title, message, acknowledged_at \
                   FROM alert_history \
                   WHERE ({severity} IS NULL OR severity = {severity}) \
                   ORDER BY fired_at DESC \
@@ -236,9 +236,10 @@ impl QueryValidator {
         self.register_template(QueryTemplate {
             name: "collector_health".to_string(),
             description: "Get collector execution status".to_string(),
-            sql: "SELECT collector_name, last_success, last_failure, success_count, failure_count \
-                  FROM collector_status \
-                  ORDER BY collector_name \
+            sql: "SELECT machine_id, collector, collected_at, success, duration_ms, \
+                  rows_inserted, error_class, freshness_seconds \
+                  FROM collector_health \
+                  ORDER BY collected_at DESC \
                   LIMIT {limit}"
                 .to_string(),
             params: vec![TemplateParam {
@@ -254,8 +255,9 @@ impl QueryValidator {
         self.register_template(QueryTemplate {
             name: "system_metrics".to_string(),
             description: "Get system metrics for a machine".to_string(),
-            sql: "SELECT machine_id, collected_at, cpu_percent, mem_percent, load5, disk_free_pct \
-                  FROM system_metrics_snapshots \
+            sql: "SELECT machine_id, collected_at, cpu_total, load1, load5, load15, \
+                  mem_used_bytes, mem_total_bytes, mem_available_bytes \
+                  FROM sys_samples \
                   WHERE ({machine_id} IS NULL OR machine_id = {machine_id}) \
                   AND collected_at >= TIMESTAMP {since} \
                   ORDER BY collected_at DESC \

@@ -58,11 +58,14 @@ CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sys_samples (
     load15 REAL,
     mem_used_bytes BIGINT,
     mem_total_bytes BIGINT,
+    mem_available_bytes BIGINT,
     swap_used_bytes BIGINT,
+    swap_total_bytes BIGINT,
     disk_read_mbps REAL,
     disk_write_mbps REAL,
     net_rx_mbps REAL,
     net_tx_mbps REAL,
+    core_count INTEGER,
     raw_json TEXT,
     PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
 );
@@ -92,22 +95,29 @@ CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sys_filesystems (
     PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, mount)
 );
 
+-- Repositories inventory
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS repos (
+    machine_id TEXT,
+    repo_id TEXT,
+    path TEXT,
+    url TEXT,
+    name TEXT,
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, repo_id)
+);
+
 -- Repository status snapshots (from ru)
 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS repo_status_snapshots (
     machine_id TEXT,
     collected_at TIMESTAMP,
-    repo_path TEXT,
-    repo_name TEXT,
+    repo_id TEXT,
     branch TEXT,
-    is_dirty BOOLEAN,
-    ahead_count INTEGER,
-    behind_count INTEGER,
-    stash_count INTEGER,
-    uncommitted_files INTEGER,
-    last_commit_ts TIMESTAMP,
-    last_commit_hash TEXT,
+    dirty BOOLEAN,
+    ahead INTEGER,
+    behind INTEGER,
+    modified_count INTEGER,
+    untracked_count INTEGER,
     raw_json TEXT,
-    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, repo_path)
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, repo_id)
 );
 
 -- Account usage snapshots (from caut)

@@ -0,0 +1,83 @@
+-- Migration 025: Add missing collector output tables
+--
+-- Tables needed by the beads, process_triage (pt), and GitHub collectors
+-- that were referenced in collector code but never created in schema.
+
+-- Beads triage snapshots (from bv --robot-triage)
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS beads_triage_snapshots (
+    machine_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
+    repo_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    quick_ref_json TEXT,
+    recommendations_json TEXT,
+    project_health_json TEXT,
+    raw_json TEXT,
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, repo_id)
+);
+
+-- Beads graph metrics (from bv graph analysis)
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS beads_graph_metrics (
+    repo_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
+    pagerank_json TEXT,
+    betweenness_json TEXT,
+    critical_path_json TEXT,
+    node_count INTEGER,
+    edge_count INTEGER,
+    density REAL,
+    has_cycles BOOLEAN,
+    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
+);
+
+-- Process triage: individual process inventory
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pt_processes (
+    machine_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
+    pid INTEGER NOT NULL,
+    name TEXT,
+    cmdline TEXT,
+    "user" TEXT,
+    started_at TEXT,
+    ended_at TEXT,
+    exit_code INTEGER,
+    status TEXT,
+    category TEXT,
+    session_id TEXT,
+    cpu_percent REAL,
+    memory_mb REAL,
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, pid)
+);
+
+-- Process triage: resource usage snapshots
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pt_snapshots (
+    machine_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
+    pid INTEGER NOT NULL,
+    snapshot_at TIMESTAMP,
+    cpu_percent REAL,
+    memory_mb REAL,
+    memory_percent REAL,
+    threads INTEGER,
+    open_files INTEGER,
+    io_read_bytes BIGINT,
+    io_write_bytes BIGINT,
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, pid)
+);
+
+-- GitHub repo issue/PR snapshots
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gh_repo_issue_pr_snapshot (
+    repo_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
+    open_issues INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
+    open_prs INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
+    triage_json TEXT,
+    label_breakdown_json TEXT,
+    raw_json TEXT,
+    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_beads_triage_machine ON beads_triage_snapshots(machine_id);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_beads_triage_collected ON beads_triage_snapshots(collected_at);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pt_processes_machine ON pt_processes(machine_id);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pt_snapshots_machine ON pt_snapshots(machine_id);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gh_snapshot_repo ON gh_repo_issue_pr_snapshot(repo_id);

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+-- Migration 026: Add status column to machines table
+--
+-- The MachineStatus enum (online/offline/unknown) is distinct from the
+-- enabled boolean. A machine can be enabled but currently offline.
+
+ALTER TABLE machines ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT DEFAULT 'unknown';
